_description: Hardware performance benchmark results for Base Llama 3.1 8B (meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B). Run with benchmark.py on the dissertation test system. Results represent the median of 5 independent prompts per configuration; each figure is the mean of three separate benchmark runs on the same day to account for thermal variation.

methodology.throughput_measurement: Five representative prompts of 20–80 tokens were passed through the model. Each prompt generated exactly 256 new tokens. The generation time was measured wall-clock (torch.cuda.synchronize() called before and after each generate() call). Tokens per second = new_tokens / elapsed_seconds. One warm-up pass of 8 tokens was discarded before each timed run. The mean and standard deviation across all five prompts are reported.
methodology.ttft_measurement: Time to First Token (TTFT) measures the prefill (encoding) phase only. The model was called with max_new_tokens=1 to isolate the prefill cost. A single warm-up pass preceded each timed measurement. Three prompt lengths were tested: short (~20 tokens), medium (~100 tokens), and long (~500 tokens). Each measurement was repeated three times and the median is reported.
methodology.vram_measurement: Peak VRAM was read with torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() after a 128-token generation pass. VRAM after model load was read before any generation.
methodology.perplexity_measurement: Computed on a 256-token passage from the WikiText-103 validation set using a sliding window of 512 tokens. Formula: exp(mean negative log-likelihood). Greedy decoding (do_sample=False) was used.
methodology.qa_probe: Five short factual prompts with known single-word/number answers were evaluated with greedy decoding (max 20 new tokens). A response was marked correct if the expected substring appeared in the generated text (case-insensitive).
methodology.reported_statistic: mean across runs, standard deviation noted where available
methodology.environment: Isolated conda environment; no other GPU workloads running during measurement. System restarted and VRAM cleared between configurations.

fp16.label: Full Precision (FP16)
fp16.description: Model weights stored in 16-bit floating point. Highest fidelity; uses the full 16 GB VRAM.

int8.label: 8-bit Quantised (INT8)
int8.description: Weights quantised to INT8 using bitsandbytes LLM.int8(). Approximately halves VRAM usage with a small quality trade-off.

int4.label: 4-bit Quantised (INT4 / NF4)
int4.description: Weights quantised to 4-bit NormalFloat (NF4) with double quantisation using bitsandbytes. Lowest VRAM footprint; fastest throughput; modest quality reduction.

summary_table.note: Extracted from full results for quick reference.
